mode: apply (COMMAND) -- PRECISION test for the SKILL.md step-5 loop-audit forcing-function (Option B). Tests over-conversion: does the skill-embedded loop audit correctly LEAVE non-convertible loops rather than blanket-converting?
constraint: HELD-OUT LEAVE-dominant target (distinct from the getTypesOfSchema recall target). Grade PRECISION = fraction of non-convertible (LEAVE) loops correctly left as loops. Any wrongful conversion of a LEAVE loop to map/filter/reduce/flatMap is a precision miss. behavior_preservation must hold (a wrongful conversion that changes behavior is the worst outcome). The prompt is neutral (names no loop/pipeline) so the skill, not the prompt, drives the audit.

targets.expected_family: Fowler mechanical clean-ups are fine (rename, extract, guard-clause). The KEY signal is NEGATIVE: the loop audit should mark every loop below LEAVE and NOT convert it.

loops.reason: worklist / consume-as-you-go: drains + mutates the same collection; assignment-in-condition; no fixed source array

loops.sig: for (path of current.subfiles) return true on .less
loops.reason: early return mid-iteration (a .some fits; a map/filter/reduce pipeline does not preserve behavior)
continue mid-iteration + side effect (pushes into the worklist being consumed)

loops.sig: for (dir of searchDirectories) if exists return true

loops.reason: multiple accumulators updated together (contentChanges, externalDependencies, needsWorkspaceIncludePath) + side-effecting predicate (I/O)
per-iteration I/O (tree.beginUpdate/commitUpdate) + multiple accumulators; source is a generator
DECOY: Set-merge, not an element transform; idiomatic fix is spread/forEach, not a map/filter/reduce pipeline
iteration-order interdependence (reverse to avoid index shifting) + side effect on updater

targets.judgment: Every side effect is an explicit tree.*/updater.* call or an accumulator mutation, so a wrongful pipeline conversion is visible in the diff. Extract/rename/guard-clause clean-ups are acceptable and not graded; only loop over-conversion and behavior changes are graded.
targets.verification: npx tsc --noEmit on the touched project + diff inspection: did any LEAVE loop become a map/filter/reduce/flatMap pipeline? did behavior change?
